title: Releases | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Securing Affordable Energy: LNG to keep the lights on and protect jobs
New Zealand households and businesses are a step closer to relief on their power bills and greater energy security, as the Government takes the next step to deliver a liquefied natural gas (LNG) import facility.

## Securing Affordable Energy: power companies made responsible for winter back-up
Kiwi households and businesses will be better protected from winter power shortages and price spikes as the Government makes the electricity sector take greater, permanent responsibility for securing the back-up power New Zealand needs.

## Ensuring safer drinking water in schools
The Government is investing $30m through the Drinking Water in Schools Programme to ensure more rural and remote schools can upgrade their drinking water, Minister of Education Erica Stanford says.

## Budget 26: $62.5 million for more classrooms in South Island Schools
Students and communities across the South Island will benefit from a $62.5 million investment package to build more classrooms and upgrade others.

## Government welcomes new diesel storage
News that work to recommission an additional 93 million litres of diesel storage capacity at Marsden Point in Northland is finished has been welcomed by the Government.

## Solomon Islands PM to visit New Zealand
Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale will visit New Zealand this week for talks with Prime Minister Christopher Luxon.

## Successful NZ-Australia PMs’ meeting concludes
Prime Minister Christopher Luxon and his Australian counterpart, Anthony Albanese, have wrapped up their Annual Leaders’ meeting with a commitment to deepening cooperation and maintaining a strong relationship.

## Paramedics to be able to prescribe medicines
Paramedics will be given the authority to prescribe medicines, enabling more patients to be treated in the community and helping reduce pressure on emergency departments, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Waste funding for Waihi Refuse Transfer Station upgrade
An upgrade of the Waihi Recycling Transfer Station that will significantly reduce waste going to landfill and boost recycling capacity has been announced.

## Ashburton community birthing unit upgrade underway
Construction is now underway on a major upgrade of the community birthing unit at Ashburton Hospital, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.
